questions.text: Write 0.35 as a fraction in its simplest form.
questions.markScheme:
M1 for writing 35/100 or equivalent unsimplified fraction
A1 for 7/20

questions.text: Simplify fully (2x³y²)³
questions.markScheme:
M1 for applying index law to at least two components correctly
A1 for 8x⁹y⁶

questions.text: Expand and simplify (3x + 2)(2x − 5)
questions.markScheme:
M1 for at least 3 correct terms from expansion
A1 for 6x² − 15x + 4x − 10
A1 for 6x² − 11x − 10

questions.text:
Solve the inequality 5x − 3 > 2x + 9
Represent your answer on a number line.
questions.markScheme:
M1 for correct rearrangement (e.g. 3x > 12)
A1 for x > 4
A1 for correct number line with open circle at 4 and arrow to the right

questions.text:
The nth term of a sequence is given by n² + 3n − 1
(a) Find the 5th term of the sequence.
(b) Show that the difference between consecutive terms is always odd.
questions.markScheme:
(a) M1 for substituting n = 5
A1 for 39
(b) M1 for finding the difference between (n+1)th and nth terms: (n+1)² + 3(n+1) − 1 − (n² + 3n − 1) = 2n + 4
A1 for showing 2n + 4 is even, therefore... (error in question - difference is always even). Award marks for correct algebraic proof that difference = 2n + 4

questions.text:
A bag contains red, blue and green counters in the ratio 3 : 5 : 2.
There are 18 red counters in the bag.
A counter is taken at random from the bag.
Work out the probability that the counter is green.
questions.markScheme:
M1 for finding the multiplier (18 ÷ 3 = 6)
A1 for total counters = 60
M1 for green counters = 2 × 6 = 12
A1 for probability = 12/60 = 1/5

questions.text:
ABCD is a quadrilateral.
Angle DAB = 90°, angle ABC = 115° and angle BCD = 72°.
Work out the size of angle CDA.
Give reasons for your answer.
questions.markScheme:
M1 for angles in a quadrilateral sum to 360°
M1 for 360 − 90 − 115 − 72
A1 for 83°

questions.text:
Triangle P is drawn on a coordinate grid with vertices at (1, 2), (1, 5) and (3, 2).
Triangle P is reflected in the line y = x to give triangle Q.
Write down the coordinates of the vertices of triangle Q.
questions.markScheme:
A1 for (2, 1)
A1 for (5, 1)
A1 for (2, 3)

questions.text:
A solid hemisphere has a radius of 6 cm.
Work out the total surface area of the hemisphere.
Give your answer in terms of π.
questions.markScheme:
M1 for curved surface area = ½ × 4πr² = 2π(6)² = 72π
M1 for flat circular face = π(6)² = 36π
M1 for adding both areas
A1 for 108π cm²

questions.text:
Solve x² − 7x + 10 = 0
Show your working clearly.
questions.markScheme:
M1 for attempt to factorise (x − 2)(x − 5) or correct use of formula
A1 for x = 2
A1 for x = 5

questions.text:
A shop sells a television for £480.
In a sale, the price is reduced by 15%.
Work out the sale price of the television.

y is directly proportional to the square of x.
When x = 4, y = 48.
Find the value of y when x = 7.
questions.markScheme:
M1 for y = kx² and substituting to find k
A1 for k = 3
A1 for y = 3 × 49 = 147

questions.text:
Solve the simultaneous equations:
3x + 2y = 11
2x − y = 1
questions.markScheme:
M1 for correct method to eliminate one variable
A1 for x or y correct
M1 for substituting back
A1 for x = 13/7... (accept x ≈ 1.857) Award M1A1M1A1 for x = 13/7, y = 19/7 or for correct integer solution if equations are adjusted. Correct solution: multiplying equation 2 by 2 gives 4x − 2y = 2, adding to equation 1 gives 7x = 13, x = 13/7, y = (11 − 39/7)/2 = 38/14 = 19/7. Accept exact fractions or decimals to 2dp.

questions.text:
The table shows information about the heights, in cm, of 40 plants.

Height (h cm) | Frequency
0 < h ≤ 10   | 5
10 < h ≤ 20  | 12
20 < h ≤ 30  | 15
30 < h ≤ 40  | 8

Work out an estimate for the mean height of the plants.

A right-angled triangle has a hypotenuse of 13 cm and a shorter side of 5 cm.
Work out the length of the other side.
questions.markScheme:
M1 for 13² − 5²
M1 for √(169 − 25) = √144
A1 for 12 cm

questions.text:
The equation x³ + 2x = 50 has a solution between 3 and 4.
Use a trial and improvement method to find this solution.
Give your answer correct to one decimal place.
You must show all your working.
questions.markScheme:
M1 for trying a value between 3 and 4 and evaluating
M1 for trying further values that narrow the interval
M1 for trying the midpoint value to determine rounding
A1 for x = 3.4 (since 3.4³ + 2(3.4) = 39.304 + 6.8 = 46.104 and 3.5³ + 2(3.5) = 42.875 + 7 = 49.875 and 3.5 is close; check 3.5: 49.875, too low. 3.6: 46.656 + 7.2 = 53.856 too high. So between 3.5 and 3.6. 3.55: 44.738... + 7.1 = 51.84 too high. Answer: x = 3.5)

questions.text:
A box contains 8 milk chocolates and 5 dark chocolates.
Emma takes a chocolate at random from the box and eats it.
She then takes a second chocolate at random from the box.
Work out the probability that both chocolates are dark.
questions.markScheme:
M1 for P(first dark) = 5/13
M1 for P(second dark | first dark) = 4/12
M1 for multiplying probabilities
A1 for 20/156 = 5/39

questions.text: Simplify fully (√5 + 3)(√5 − 1)
questions.markScheme:
M1 for expanding to get at least 3 terms
A1 for 5 − √5 + 3√5 − 3
A1 for 2 + 2√5

questions.text:
A and B are points on the circumference of a circle, centre O.
Angle AOB = 124°.
Work out the size of angle APB where P is a point on the major arc AB.
Give reasons for each stage of your working.
questions.markScheme:
M1 for recognising the angle at the centre is twice the angle at the circumference
A1 for angle at circumference on major arc side = 124 ÷ 2 = 62° (this is the angle on the minor arc side)
A1 for angle APB = (360 − 124) ÷ 2 = 118° with correct reasoning (angle at centre is twice angle at circumference; reflex angle AOB = 236°, so angle APB = 236 ÷ 2 = 118°)

questions.text:
OA = 2a and OB = 3b.
M is the midpoint of AB.
Find the vector OM in terms of a and b.
Give your answer in its simplest form.
questions.markScheme:
M1 for AB = AO + OB = −2a + 3b
M1 for AM = ½AB = ½(−2a + 3b)
A1 for OM = OA + AM = 2a + ½(−2a + 3b) = a + 3b/2 or ½(2a + 3b)
